rewrites.newDefinition: Lingering in one place, expecting someone or something to arrive.
Bright star warming the sky, central to weather small talk.
Coastal seabird with white feathers, screeching loudly above harbors and piers.
Folded sheets of daily printed news, sold each morning at kiosks.
Glass-paneled opening in a wall, letting in light and view.
Bound printed pages read for pleasure, study, or quiet escape.
Span of seven consecutive days marking time between recurring events.
To hold a job, doing tasks in exchange for pay.
Retail shop selling new books, often with knowledgeable staff recommendations.
Pressed dairy made from milk, central to German breakfast tables.
Curved bony joint connecting torso to leg, often aching with age.
Younger generation descended from one's children, observing family rituals fade.
Independent shop selling new and curated books to neighborhood readers.
Folded paper sleeve sealing letters, contracts, and important offers inside.
Regular monthly pay received from an employer for steady work.
Carrying weight, literally on a scale or figuratively on conscience.
Hesitant and tentative, lacking confidence in each careful brushstroke.
Trusted advisor offering opinions, sometimes the inner critic disrupting flow.
Bristled tool dipped in paint, transferring color onto waiting canvas.
Young female child, grammatically neuter due to its diminutive ending.
Series of steps connecting one floor to another inside buildings.
Thick dark plum spread, slowly cooked down with warm autumn spices.
Secret plot among multiple parties, often invoked playfully about coincidences.
Annual celebration marking the day someone was born, with cake.
Small storefront selling goods, often independently owned within a neighborhood.
Small printed rectangle: greeting, postcard, ticket, or playing card.
Forming letters or words with pen, marking thoughts onto paper.
